questions.question: How much of the year does the prohibition of gas leaf blowers last? What date in the year does it start, and what date does it end?
questions.answer: The prohibition of gas-powered leaf blowers lasts from June 15 to September 15 each year. It also prohibits their use on Saturdays, Sundays, and holidays from September 16 to June 14 each year.

questions.question: What are the permitted hours for gas leaf blower operation?
questions.answer: Gas leaf blowers are permitted to be operated between the hours of 8:00 a.m. and 5:00 p.m. from June 16 to September 14. They are not allowed to be used at all on Saturdays, Sundays, and holidays from September 16 to June 14.

questions.question: What are the penalties for violating gas leaf blower regulations?
questions.answer: The penalties for violating gas leaf blower regulations are provided in Chapter 1, General Provisions, Article II, but specific details of the penalties are not detailed in § 205.

questions.question: Are there any exceptions for school/government/commercial properties?
